content.claim: Hungary operates three parallel entry points to early-selective academic tracks (gimnáziums) at ages 10, 12, and 14, with approximately 22,000–25,000 students enrolled across ~200 schools, representing a small but politically salient share of the student population.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Education finance lens
limitations: Based on expert estimates rather than administrative census data
Exact enrollment figures require verification from Hungarian Ministry of Education records
Does not capture recent trend changes in enrollment
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
